title: Chat intent-detection capture
description: Extend the chat turn classifier with a capture-task action and wire the chat session to act on it. A new-task statement ("renew passport") captures a task via captureTask instead of being answered; a question still routes to answer; a scope-statement ("I'm deciding between X and Y") does NOT capture a task.

successCriteria.description: `npm run build` exits 0 with the capture-task classifier action and the chat-session handler branch added.
A unit test feeds the classifier (with a stubbed LLM returning the capture-task JSON) an imperative task phrase and asserts it parses to {action:'capture-task', task:<text>}.
A unit test asserts a question input yields {action:'answer'} and a scope-statement input does NOT yield capture-task (it maps to answer), so prioritization phrasing is not mistaken for a task.

A unit test drives the chat-session handler with a capture-task action and an in-memory FactStore, asserting one open action-Finding is persisted and the user-facing turn is a capture confirmation rather than an LLM answer.
A unit test confirms a malformed classifier response falls back to {action:'answer'} (no throw), preserving the existing FALLBACK contract.

nonGoals: Do NOT change any existing classifier action (answer/spawn/steer/approve/reject/tell/pause/resume) behavior — only ADD the capture-task variant.
Do NOT remove the parse-failure FALLBACK to {action:'answer'} (turn-classifier.ts:46,118).
Do NOT make capture depend on an LLM round-trip for the actual write — the handler calls captureTask directly once the intent is known.
Do NOT alter the approval/steer/pause subsystems.
stopConditions: `npm run build` exits 0 and the classifier + chat-session capture unit tests pass.
A capture-task turn persists a Finding and returns a confirmation, while question and scope-statement turns do not capture a task.
definitionOfDone: Typing a plain task into chat (e.g. "renew passport") is recognized as a new-task intent and captured into the hub pool as an open action-Finding, with the chat reply being a short capture confirmation. Questions still route to the answerer and scope-statements (decision framing) are not captured as tasks. The classifier keeps its never-throw fallback to answer on any parse failure.
assumptions: The capture-task action is added to the ClassifierAction discriminated union and schema in src/chat/turn-classifier.ts as {action:'capture-task', task:string}, with the system prompt extended to describe it and to explicitly distinguish a decision/scope-statement (route to answer) from a new task.

The chat session (src/chat/chat-session.ts) gains a branch that, on capture-task, opens the FactStore, calls captureTask with now stamped at the boundary, and emits a confirmation turn instead of invoking the Answerer.
captureTask from sprint 1 is the single write path reused here.
outOfScope: Scoped prioritization commands like /today,/decide (priority-hub + telegram scope).
generatorNotes: In src/chat/turn-classifier.ts: add z.object({action:z.literal('capture-task'), task:z.string()}) to ClassifierActionSchema (turn-classifier.ts:24-44), add the matching union member to ClassifierAction (lines 11-20), handle it in parseClassifierAction (after the spawn branch ~line 90), and extend the system prompt option list (lines 141-156) to include capture-task AND a note that 'I'm deciding between X and Y' / scope statements route to answer, not capture-task. Keep FALLBACK={action:'answer'} unchanged. In src/chat/chat-session.ts: where classifier actions are dispatched, add a capture-task case that resolves the project root + namespace, opens FactStore(factsDbPath(root,ns)) after ensureFactsDir, calls captureTask(store, action.task, {now:new Date().toISOString()}), closes the store, and records a confirmation turn (e.g. 'Captured task: <text>') via the existing conversation/turn mechanism rather than calling the Answerer. Mirror the existing dispatch/try-catch style already in chat-session.ts.
evaluatorNotes: Confirm only an ADDITIVE union member was introduced and every existing action still parses (run/inspect the existing turn-classifier tests — they must stay green). Verify the scope-statement test does NOT classify as capture-task. Verify the chat-session capture branch writes via captureTask (reused, not a new write path) and emits a confirmation turn, not an Answerer call. Confirm the malformed-response FALLBACK still returns answer.
